meta.sprintTitle: Template Layout System v1 - Canonical Layout Schema + Data-Driven Studio Population
meta.goal: Replace hardcoded page layout geometry with a canonical template layout system so page composition, Studio rendering, and exports all use the same reusable layout definitions.

basedOnPriorSprints: Sprint 6 - WYSIWYG Page Layout System v1
Sprint 7 - Layout to PDF Renderer v1
Sprint 21 - Chapter to Studio Populate v1
Sprint 50 - Admin Console v1 - Template Management
whyNow: Current chapter population still relies on hardcoded fallback slot geometry and generated page layouts in application code
Template metadata exists, but layout definitions are not yet a first-class versioned data model
A visual builder, preview rendering, and per-page client layout switching all depend on a canonical layout schema first
productContext: Memorioso needs layout fidelity between Studio and export
Template-driven storybooks should be editable without redeploying frontend layout code
Each page should eventually be able to swap to a different compatible layout while preserving mapped content

nonGoals: Full visual drag-and-drop template builder UI in this sprint
Bulk migration of all historical books to new layouts
dependencies: Existing pages and frames data model
PDF renderer contract and page/frame renderer

operational: At least one production template can be rendered without relying on hardcoded fallback slot placement
Studio population reads layout data from template JSON rather than code constants
A layout definition can be updated without frontend redeploys for geometry-only changes
product: Template layouts become reusable across multiple storybooks and chapters
Layout definitions can represent title, body, quote, caption, image, and decorative slots precisely
Future layout picker and builder work can extend the same schema without redesign
quality: Studio and export use the same coordinates and slot definitions
Validation blocks malformed or incomplete layouts from being published
Migration path exists for current hardcoded chapter layouts
security: Only authorised admins can modify template layout definitions
Layout JSON is validated server-side before persistence or publish
Existing books remain stable even if a layout is later edited

personas.name: Content Admin
personas.description: Needs canonical, editable layout definitions that control how chapters and pages are visually assembled.

personas.name: Studio User
personas.description: Sees template-driven pages rendered consistently and should benefit from reduced layout bugs.

personas.name: Renderer/System Owner
personas.description: Needs one layout source of truth across Studio and export.

inScope: Page layout and slot entities inside template JSON
Data-driven Studio population from template layouts
Migration of current hardcoded fallback layouts into seeded layout JSON
Validation for slot ids, geometry, slot types, and compatibility metadata
Stable references from page instances to selected page layout ids
outOfScope: Advanced dynamic layout reflow engine beyond current compatibility flags

architecture.recommendedApproach: Promote page layouts to first-class template data. Each template owns reusable page layout definitions and chapter page plans. Studio population resolves chapter content into named slots within those layouts, creates concrete frames from the layout definition, and stores a pageLayoutId reference on the page instance.
principles: Template layout is data, not frontend code
Geometry should be represented once and reused everywhere
Slot mapping must remain stable across layout edits and future page switching
Existing storybooks should pin to the layout version used when populated unless explicitly migrated

coreEntities.purpose: Top-level visual layout package associated with a template version
A reusable page-level layout with page size assumptions, slot set, and compatibility metadata
A positioned image/text/shape slot with x, y, w, h, zIndex, type, and rules
Reference from a concrete storybook page to the layout used to create or render it

functionalRequirements.title: Canonical layout schema
requirements: Define a typed layout schema for page layouts and slots
Support text, image, frame, shape, line, and decorative slots where relevant
Allow explicit coordinates and dimensions in the same space used by pages and frames

functionalRequirements.title: Template-owned reusable page layouts
requirements: A template can include multiple reusable page layouts
Layouts can be identified by stable pageLayoutId values
Layouts can declare page role such as chapter opener, story page, gallery page, quote page, or cover

functionalRequirements.title: Chapter page plans
requirements: Templates can declare which page layouts are used for a chapter and in what sequence
Population can resolve a chapter page plan without falling back to hardcoded geometry
Plans can reference alternative layout ids for portrait and landscape where needed

functionalRequirements.title: Data-driven Studio population
requirements: Studio population must read slots from template layout definitions
Hardcoded fallback page specs should be retained only as a temporary migration safety net and not as the primary path
Created frames must include stable slot metadata derived from pageLayoutId and slotId

functionalRequirements.title: Page instance layout references
requirements: Concrete storybook pages must store which layout they were created from
The stored layout reference should be usable by preview, export, and future page layout switching
Layout references must survive reload and export jobs

functionalRequirements.title: Validation
requirements: Validate duplicate slot ids, invalid geometry, unsupported slot kinds, and incompatible orientation flags
Validate that required text/image slots referenced by chapter mappings actually exist
Block publishing when layouts are malformed

functionalRequirements.title: Migration of current layouts
requirements: Seed the current hardcoded chapter layout and answer-page geometry as explicit template layouts
Preserve the current visual output while moving geometry into data
Document the migration and deprecation of code-defined layouts

recommendedEndpoints.purpose: Return page layouts and chapter page plans for a template
Update canonical layout JSON for a template draft
Validate layout schema and publish readiness

technicalTasks.title: Define layout schema contract
technicalTasks.description: Create typed contracts for page layouts, slots, chapter page plans, and layout validation.

technicalTasks.title: Extend template storage
technicalTasks.description: Add canonical layout fields to template JSON and Convex read/write paths.

technicalTasks.title: Refactor Studio population to use layout definitions
technicalTasks.description: Remove primary dependence on hardcoded page specs and generate frames from stored page layouts.

technicalTasks.title: Persist page layout references on pages
technicalTasks.description: Store selected page layout ids and source template version information on concrete page instances.

technicalTasks.title: Seed current hardcoded layouts into data
technicalTasks.description: Translate existing fallback chapter layout and generated answer layouts into seeded template layout JSON.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add tests covering schema validation, population parity, and layout references.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a published template with canonical page layouts
acceptanceCriteria.then: frames are created from the stored layout definitions instead of hardcoded fallback geometry

acceptanceCriteria.when: it is read from the system
acceptanceCriteria.then: its selected pageLayoutId and source template version are available

acceptanceCriteria.when: an admin validates or publishes the template
acceptanceCriteria.then: the action is blocked with clear schema errors
Studio and export output remain visually equivalent within acceptable tolerance

risks.risk: Layout schema becomes too narrow for future page switching
risks.mitigation: Model reusable page layouts and slot compatibility now instead of chapter-only hardcoded pages

risks.risk: Migration changes current page output unexpectedly
risks.mitigation: Create fixture-based parity checks against current hardcoded layouts before removing them

risks.risk: Template JSON grows without clear validation
risks.mitigation: Use strict typed contracts and publish-time validation

definitionOfDone: Canonical template layout schema exists and is documented
Templates can store page layouts and chapter page plans
Studio population reads stored layouts as the primary path
Concrete pages store pageLayoutId and template layout provenance
Current hardcoded layouts are seeded into data or clearly isolated as migration-only fallback
Automated tests cover schema validation and population parity
